experience.organization: Structures & Artificial Intelligence Lab (SAIL), University of Houston
responsibilities: Develop Python programs to process RGB-D sensor data for computer vision research.
Build data pipelines that clean, organize, and prepare sensor data for machine learning experiments.
Train and test PyTorch deep learning models for depth refinement and 3D reconstruction.
Create synthetic training data using Blender and Mitsuba 3 to improve model evaluation.
Add sensor noise simulations to study how models perform under real-world conditions.
Use Linux computing clusters to run larger experiments and model training jobs.
Write object-oriented Python code to create reusable research tools and workflows.
Use Claude Code and Codex to help prototype ideas, debug code, and document workflows while reviewing and testing all outputs.
Debug problems in data processing, model training, and experiment setup.
Run experiments, compare results, and make changes based on findings.
Document research processes so experiments can be repeated and extended.
Present weekly progress updates and discuss results with researchers.

projects.name: CodeRED Eco
responsibilities: Built a web application that estimates product environmental impact using AI-generated insights.
Integrated the Google Gemini API to analyze product information and generate sustainability recommendations.
Created interactive charts using Recharts to display environmental impact information.
Collaborated with teammates using GitHub branches, commits, and pull requests.
Used Claude Code and Codex to help prototype features, debug issues, and improve development speed while validating changes manually.
Followed the software development process from planning and design to implementation, testing, and deployment.
Changed the original Chrome extension idea into a web application after discovering limitations with Amazon product data access.

projects.name: Depth Refinement Neural Network
responsibilities: Develop machine learning models to improve depth information from RGB-D sensors.
Process and analyze image and depth data for computer vision experiments.
Generate synthetic datasets using Blender and Mitsuba 3.
Train and evaluate models using different datasets and experiment settings.
Test model performance and make improvements based on results.

projects.name: Driver Drowsiness Detection System
responsibilities: Built a computer vision application that detects signs of driver fatigue.
Used facial landmarks and image processing to analyze driver behavior.
Tested and debugged detection logic to improve reliability.
